runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 5 in a U.S. microsimulation model.
citations: Standard labor supply elasticities often estimated as positive and moderate in mid-income groups
Microsimulation tax-benefit responses typically show stronger substitution effects when incentives change but with substantial attenuation by institutional features
Decile-specific elasticities commonly vary by participation/marital status composition and by baseline income-to-benefit phaseout regimes
runs.reasoningSummary: I expect a positive but moderate substitution-response elasticity for primary earners in the 5th earnings decile: incentives changes matter, yet are moderated by budget-set constraints, extensive-margin participation effects, and tax-credit phaseout/structure. Hence a median around 0.6 with a plausible range from roughly 0.25 to 1.2.

Hence a median around 0.6 with a plausible range from roughly 0.25 to 1.2."}
